# Page: /[username]/[eventSlug]

## Purpose
**Public, unauthenticated** "vanity URL" booking page — the link generated by the "Share Booking Link" dialog on `/meetings/event-types` (`{origin}/{username}/{slug-or-id}`). Functionally identical to [public-booking-by-event-type](./public-booking-by-event-type.md); the event type is resolved via the `eventSlug` route param (fetched as `event-types/{eventSlug}`, which works whether the value is a slug or a raw ID) instead of a dedicated `eventTypeId` param. The `username` param is read but not sent to the backend in the fetch call — it is present purely for URL aesthetics/branding.

## Components used
Identical to `/book/[eventTypeId]`: `src/components/ui/*` primitives (Button, Card, `CustomCalendar`, Avatar, Input, Label, Textarea, RadioGroup, Checkbox, Select, Dialog), all step/state logic inline in the page component.

## Data/API calls
Same pattern as the sibling page, direct `fetch()` to `API_BASE_URL` (`@/lib/api`), no auth header:
- `GET {API_BASE_URL}/event-types/{eventSlug}` — load event type
- `GET {API_BASE_URL}/event-types/{id}/availability?date&timezone&duration&buffer_before&buffer_after` — available slots
- `POST {API_BASE_URL}/bookings` — create booking

## Notable behavior
- Same security note as the sibling page: no auth, no CSRF, publicly reachable and enumerable by username/slug.
- Same `?embed=true` support, same `isDateAvailable` day-of-week-first logic, same per-question validation via `validateQuestionResponse`, same 422/"slot taken" race handling, same optional `redirect_url` auto-redirect on success.
- Because this file and `/book/[eventTypeId]/page.tsx` are near-line-for-line duplicates, any future bug fix or scheduling-logic change likely needs to be applied to both.
- Cross-reference: end-to-end flow documented in [meeting-scheduling-public-flow](../flows/meeting-scheduling-public-flow.md).
